heading: Disclosure of reportable transactions
label: 6111 Disclosure of reportable transactions
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 6111 was renumbered 6116 of this title.

Amendments2005—Subsec. (b)(1)(A)(ii). substituted “aid, assistance, or advice” for “advice or assistance”.Pub. L. 109–1352004— amended section catchline and text generally, substituting provisions relating to disclosure of reportable transactions for provisions relating to registration of tax shelters and inclusion of identification numbers on returns.Pub. L. 108–3571997—Subsecs. (d) to (f). added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsecs. (d) and (e) as (e) and (f), respectively.Pub. L. 105–341986—Subsec. (c)(2)(A). , substituted “350 percent” for “200 percent”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1531(a)Subsec. (c)(3)(B)(ii). , substituted “section 465(b)(3)(C)” for “section 168(e)(4)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 201(d)(13)Subsec. (d)(1)(B). , substituted “subparagraph” for “subpargraph”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(54)

Effective Date of 2004 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–357, title VIII, § 815(c)118 Stat. 1583Oct. 22, 2004“The amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 6112 and 6708 of this title] shall apply to transactions with respect to which material aid, assistance, or advice referred to in section 6111(b)(1)(A)(i) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by this section) is provided after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”Oct. 22, 2004

Effective Date of 1997 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–34, title X, § 1028(e)111 Stat. 928Aug. 5, 1997“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 6662 and 6707 of this title] shall apply to any tax shelter (as defined in section 6111(d) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended by this section) interests in which are offered to potential participants after the Secretary of the Treasury prescribes guidance with respect to meeting requirements added by such amendments.“(2).—Modifications to substantial understatement penaltyThe amendments made by subsection (c) [amending ] shall apply to items with respect to transactions entered into after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 6662 of this titleAug. 5, 1997

Effective Date of 1986 AmendmentAmendment by applicable to property placed in service after , in taxable years ending after such date, with exceptions, see sections 203 and 204 of , set out as a note under .section 201(d)(13) of Pub. L. 99–514Pub. L. 99–514section 168 of this titleDec. 31, 1986Amendment by not applicable to any property placed in service before , if such property placed in service as part of specified rehabilitations, and not applicable to certain additional rehabilitations, see section 251(d)(2), (3) of , set out as a note under .section 201(d)(13) of Pub. L. 99–514Pub. L. 99–514section 46 of this titleJan. 1, 1994, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XV, § 1531(b)100 Stat. 2749Oct. 22, 1986“The amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to any tax shelter (within the meaning of section 6111 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] as amended by this section) interests in which are first offered for sale after .”December 31, 1986

Effective Date, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 141(d)98 Stat. 680Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting this section and and renumbering former section 6111 as ] shall apply to any tax shelter (within the meaning of section 6111 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], as added by this section) any interest in which is first sold to any investor after .section 6707 of this titlesection 6112 of this titleAugust 31, 1984“(2).—Substantial investment testFor purposes of determining whether any investment is a tax shelter by reason of section 6111(c)(1)(B)(iii) of such Code (as added by this section), only offers for sale after , shall be taken into account.August 31, 1984“(3).—Furnishing of shelter identification number for interests sold beforeseptember 1, 1984With respect to interests sold before , any liability to act under paragraph (1) of section 6111(b) of such Code (as added by this section) which would (but for this sentence) arise before such date shall be deemed to arise on .”September 1, 1984December 31, 1984

children.text: information identifying and describing the transaction,
information describing any potential tax benefits expected to result from the transaction, and
such other information as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.label: i
children.text: who provides any material aid, assistance, or advice with respect to organizing, managing, promoting, selling, implementing, insuring, or carrying out any reportable transaction, and

children.label: ii
children.text: who directly or indirectly derives gross income in excess of the threshold amount (or such other amount as may be prescribed by the Secretary) for such aid, assistance, or advice.

children.label: i
children.text: $50,000 in the case of a reportable transaction substantially all of the tax benefits from which are provided to natural persons, and

children.heading: Reportable transaction
children.label: 2 Reportable transaction
children.text: The term “reportable transaction” has the meaning given to such term by section 6707A(c).
that only 1 person shall be required to meet the requirements of subsection (a) in cases in which 2 or more persons would otherwise be required to meet such requirements,
exemptions from the requirements of this section, and
such rules as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.
